options.tooltip:
Sets the maximum life of the target corpse for Detonate Dead and similar skills.
For reference, a level 70 monster has 0 base life, and a level 80 monster has 0.
options.label: Enemy Corpse Life:

options.tooltip: Applies mods that use `while stationary` and `per / every second while stationary`
options.label: Time spent stationary
Are you always on Full Life?
Are you always on Low Life?
Are you always on Full Mana?

options.tooltip:
You will automatically be considered to be on Low Mana if you have at least 50% mana reserved,
but you can use this option to force it if necessary.
options.label: Are you always on Low Mana?
Are you always on Full Energy Shield?

options.tooltip:
You will automatically be considered to be on Low Energy Shield if you have at least 50% ES reserved,
but you can use this option to force it if necessary.
options.label: Are you always on Low Energy Shield?
Do you always have Energy Shield?
Are your Minions always on Full Life?
Minion is always on Full Energy Shield?
Have your Minions been created Recently?

options.tooltip:
Controls how the base damage for applying Ailments is calculated:
	Average: damage is based on the average application, including both crits and non-crits
	Crits Only: damage is based solely on Ailments inflicted with crits
options.label: Ailment calculation mode:

options.tooltip:
Controls how the cooldown for skills is calculated:
	Base: The cooldown is calculated with normal behavior.
	Average: The cooldown is adjusted to reflect the average time between uses, factoring in effects such as a chance to not consume a cooldown.
options.label: Cooldown calculation mode:

options.tooltip:
Controls how modifiers which choose a random element will function.
	Average: Modifiers will grant one third of their value to Fire, Cold, and Lightning simultaneously
	Fire / Cold / Lightning: Modifiers will grant their full value as the specified element
If a modifier chooses between just two elements, the full value can only be given as those two elements.
options.label: Random element mode:

options.tooltip:
Controls how Life regeneration is calculated:
	Minimum: does not include burst regen
	Average: includes burst regen, averaged based on uptime
	Burst: includes full burst regen
options.label: Life regen calculation mode:

options.tooltip:
Controls how resource on hit/kill is calculated:
	Minimum: does not include chances
	Average: includes chance gains, averaged based on uptime
	Maximum: treats all chances as certain
options.label: Resource gain calculation mode:

options.tooltip:
Sets the EHP calc to pretend its unlucky and reduce the effects of random events such as
	Block/Spell Block Chance
	Dodge/Spell Dodge Chance
	Spell Suppression Chance
	Avoidance Chance
options.label: EHP calc unlucky:

options.tooltip: Sets the EHP calc to not apply gain on block and suppress effects
options.label: Disable EHP gain on block/suppress:

options.tooltip:
Controls how Defending with Double Armour is calculated:
	Minimum: never Defend with Double Armour
	Average: Damage Reduction from Defending with Double Armour is proportional to chance
	Maximum: always Defend with Double Armour
This setting has no effect if you have 100% chance to Defend with Double Armour.
options.label: Armour calculation mode:

options.tooltip:
Controls how exerted attacks from Warcries are calculated:
Average: Averages out Warcry usage with cast time, attack speed and warcry cooldown.
Max Hit: Shows maximum hit for lining up all warcries.
options.label: Exerted/Boosted calc mode:

options.tooltip: Ignore the effects of things which disable items, like Bringer of Rain
options.label: Don't disable items

options.tooltip: Ignore the limits on jewels
options.label: Ignore Jewel Limits

options.tooltip:
When enabled, the mana spent by Arcane Cloak used at full mana 
will be added to the value provided in # of Mana spent Recently.
options.label: Include in Mana spent Recently?

options.tooltip: Amount of Red, Blue & Purple Flames consumed during duration (max 10)
options.label: Chayula Breach Flames:

options.tooltip: The amount of valour consumed for the placed banner
options.label: Banner Valour:

Cruelty is a buff provided by Cruelty Support which grants

options.label: Are you in Demon Form?

options.tooltip:
Glorious Madness Stacks grants:
	Eroding Touch: 6% inc Damage Taken per stack
	Paralysing Touch: 6% reduced Action Speed per stack
	Diluting Touch: 9% reduced Flask charges gained and 9% reduced Flask Effect per stack
	Wasting Touch: 9% reduced Life and Energy Shield recovery rate per stack
options.label: Glorious Madness Stacks

Buff affects the player and allies
Buff has a base duration of 8s with a 10s Cooldown

Harbinger of Time Slipstream buff grants:
10% increased Action Speed
Buff affects the player, allies and enemies in a small radius
Buff has a base duration of 8s with a 20s Cooldown

options.tooltip:
Power determines how strong your Warcry buffs will be, and is based on the total strength of nearby enemies.
Power is assumed to be 20 if your target is a Boss, but you can override it here if necessary.
	Each Normal enemy grants 1 Power
	Each Magic enemy grants 2 Power
	Each Rare enemy grants 10 Power
	Each Unique enemy grants 20 Power
options.label: Warcry Power:

options.label: Whirlwind gained Cold Element:

options.tooltip: Whirlwind has passed over Fire Ground.

options.tooltip: Skills can be temporarily Empowered by certain buff skills.
options.label: Is the skill Empowered?

options.tooltip:
Your Absolution Skill Damage will not be scaled with Count setting.
By default it multiplies both minion count and skill hit count which leads to incorrect
Total DPS calculation since Absolution cannot inherently shotgun.
Do not enable if you use Spell Totem support, Spell Cascade support or similar supports
options.label: Absolution: Count skill damage once

options.tooltip:
Molten Shell reflects damage to the enemy,
based on the amount of damage it has mitigated.
options.label: Damage mitigated:

options.tooltip:
Vaal Molten Shell reflects damage to the enemy,
based on the amount of damage it has mitigated in the last second.
options.label: Damage mitigated:

options.tooltip:
Configure the radius of an enemy hitbox which is used in calculating some area multi-hitting (shotgunning) effects.

Small sets the radius to 2.
	Most monsters and the players' character are this size.
Medium sets the radius to 3.
	This is the size of most humanoid bosses (i.e. The Maven; The Shaper; Izaro)
Large sets the radius to 5.
	This is the size of some larger bosses (i.e. King Kaom; Vaal Oversoul)
Huge sets the radius to 11.
	This is the size of some of the largest bosses (i.e. Nucleus of the Maven; Tsoagoth, The Brine King)
options.label: Enemy size preset:

options.tooltip: Configure the radius of an enemy hitbox to calculate some area overlapping (shotgunning) effects.
options.label: Enemy radius:

options.tooltip: The total life of your Spectres that can be taken before yours (used by jinxed juju)
options.label: Total Spectre Life:

options.tooltip: The total life of your Vaal Rejuvenation Totems that can be taken before yours

options.tooltip:
Controls how the hit/ailment effect of Ruthless Support is calculated:
	Average: damage is based on the average application
	Max Effect: damage is based on maximum effect
options.label: Ruthless Support Mode:

options.tooltip:
Controls how the chance on hit to ignore enemy physical damage reduction is calculated:
	Minimum: never ignores unless chance is at least 100%
	Average: damage is based on the average application
	Max Effect: always ignores if you have any chance
options.label: Chance To Ignore PDR Mode:

options.tooltip:
Some Skills, Items, Support Gems and other effects can Break Armour, which lowers a target's Armour by a specified amount.
If this brings the target's Armour value to 0, their Armour is Fully Broken for 12 seconds.
options.label: Is enemy Armour Broken?

options.tooltip:
Use this field to set a custom Armour Break value.
If left empty or set to 0, Fully Broken Armour will be assumed.
Armour cannot be broken below 0 unless stated otherwise.
If so, Armour can be broken up to the inverse of its original value.
options.label: # of Broken Armour (if not maximum):

options.tooltip:
The enemy will automatically be considered to be Cursed if you have at least one curse enabled,
but you can use this option to force it if necessary.
options.label: Is the enemy Cursed?

options.tooltip: Dazed enemies take 50% more stun buildup for 8 seconds.
options.label: Is the enemy Dazed?
# of Bleeds on enemy (if not average):

options.tooltip: Rupture applies more bleed damage and faster bleeds for 3 seconds, up to 3 stacks
options.label: # of Rupture stacks?

options.tooltip: Incision applies 10% chance to Bleed the enemy, up to 10 stacks.
options.label: # of Incision Stacks:
# of Poisons on enemy (if not average):

options.tooltip: Withered applies 5% increased Chaos Damage Taken to the enemy, up to 10 stacks.
options.label: # of Withered Stacks:

options.tooltip:
Each stack of Corrosion applies -5000 to total Armour and -1000 to total Evasion Rating to the enemy.
Corrosion lasts 4 seconds and refreshes the duration of existing Corrosion stacks
Corrosion has no stack limit
options.label: # of Corrosion Stacks:

options.tooltip:
While ensnared, enemies take increased Projectile Damage from Attack Hits
Ensnared enemies always count as moving, and have less movement speed while trying to break the snare.
options.label: # of Ensnare Stacks:

options.tooltip:
In addition to allowing 'against Maimed Enemies' modifiers to apply,
 Maim applies the following effects.
 -30% Reduced Movement Speed 
 -15% Reduced Evasion
options.label: Is the enemy Maimed?

options.tooltip:
In addition to allowing 'against Blinded Enemies' modifiers to apply,
 Blind applies the following effects.
 -20% Less Accuracy 
 -20% Less Evasion
options.label: Is the enemy Blinded?

options.tooltip: If you have a guaranteed source of Blind, the strongest one will apply.
options.label: Effect of Blind (if not maximum):

options.tooltip:
Pinned enemies cannot move and cannot evade 4 seconds.
 They are also Light Stunned when they become pinned.
options.label: Is the enemy Pinned?

options.tooltip: Debilitated enemies deal 10% less damage.
options.label: Is the enemy Debilitated?

options.tooltip: A target is immobilised if it cannot move, for example due to being Frozen, Pinned, Heavy Stunned, or Electrocuted.
options.label: Is the enemy Immobilised?

options.tooltip: Enemies are Pacified after 60% of Pacify's duration has expired
options.label: Is the enemy Pacified?

options.tooltip: This also implies that the enemy is Burning.
options.label: Is the enemy Ignited?
# of Ignites on enemy (if not average):

options.tooltip:
Scorched enemies have lowered elemental resistances, up to -30%.
This option will also allow you to input the effect of Scorched.
options.label: Is the enemy Scorched?

options.tooltip: This effect will only be applied while you can inflict Scorched.
options.label: Effect of Scorched:

options.tooltip: Amount of stacks of Scorch applied to the enemy.
options.label: Scorch Stacks

options.tooltip: This also implies that the enemy is Scorched.
options.label: Is the enemy on Scorched Ground?

options.tooltip: Amount of stacks of Chill applied to the enemy.
options.label: Chill Stacks
Is the enemy Chilled by your Hits?

options.tooltip: Amount of stacks of Hoarfrost applied to the enemy.
options.label: Hoarfrost Stacks

options.tooltip: This also implies that the enemy is Chilled.
options.label: Is the enemy on Chilled Ground?

options.tooltip:
Hits against Brittle enemies have up to +6% Critical Strike Chance.
This option will also allow you to input the effect of Brittle.
options.label: Is the enemy Brittle?

options.tooltip: This effect will only be applied while you can inflict Brittle.
options.label: Effect of Brittle:

options.tooltip: This also implies that the enemy is Brittle.
options.label: Is the enemy on Brittle Ground?

options.tooltip:
In addition to allowing any 'against Shocked Enemies' modifiers to apply,
this will allow you to input the effect of the Shock applied to the enemy.
options.label: Is the enemy Shocked?

options.tooltip:
If you have a guaranteed source of Shock,
the strongest one will apply instead unless this option would apply a stronger Shock.
options.label: Effect of Shock (if not maximum):

options.tooltip: Amount of stacks of Shock applied to the enemy.
options.label: Shock Stacks

options.tooltip: This also implies that the enemy is Shocked.
options.label: Is the enemy on Shocked Ground?

options.tooltip: Sapped enemies deal less damage, up to 20%.
options.label: Is the enemy Sapped?

options.tooltip:
If you have a guaranteed source of Sap,
the strongest one will apply instead unless this option would apply a stronger Sap.
options.label: Effect of Sap:

options.tooltip: This also implies that the enemy is Sapped.
options.label: Is the enemy on Sapped Ground?
# of Freeze / Shock / Ignite on enemy:

options.tooltip: This applies -20% Fire Resistance to the enemy.
options.label: Is the enemy Exposed to Fire?

options.tooltip: This applies -20% Cold Resistance to the enemy.
options.label: Is the enemy Exposed to Cold?

options.tooltip: This applies -20% Lightning Resistance to the enemy.
options.label: Is the enemy Exposed to Lightning?

options.tooltip: Intimidate is a Debuff that inflicts 10% increased damage taken and 10% reduced damage dealt.
options.label: Is the enemy Intimidated?

options.tooltip: Crushed enemies have 15% reduced Physical Damage Reduction.
options.label: Is the enemy Crushed?
Is the enemy near you Linked target?

options.tooltip: Unnerved enemies take 10% increased Spell Damage.
options.label: Is the enemy Unnerved?

options.tooltip:
Hits against Critically Weak enemies have up to +10% Critical Strike Chance.
This option will also allow you to input the effect of Critical Weakness.
options.label: Is the enemy Critically Weak

options.tooltip: Each stack of critical weakness applies +0.5% to critical strike chance against the affected target
options.label: Critical weakness stacks:

options.tooltip:
Covered in Ash applies the following to the enemy:
	20% increased Fire Damage taken
	20% less Movement Speed
options.label: Is the enemy covered in Ash?

options.tooltip:
Covered in Frost applies the following to the enemy:
	20% increased Cold Damage taken
	50% less Critical Strike Chance
options.label: Is the enemy covered in Frost?
Is the enemy on Consecrated Ground?
Does the enemy have Energy Shield?

options.tooltip:
Enemies on Profane Ground receive the following modifiers:
	10% increased Effect of Curses
	100% increased chance to be Critically Hit
options.label: Is the enemy on Profane Ground?

options.tooltip: Enemies on your Fungal Ground have -10% to all Resistances.
options.label: Is the enemy on Fungal Ground?
Is the enemy in a Chilling area?
Is the enemy in the Frost Shield area?
Enemy was Hit by Fire Damage?
Enemy was Hit by Cold Damage?
Enemy was Hit by Light. Damage?
Is the enemy in RF or Scorching Ray:

options.tooltip: This option prevents EE from being reset by the hit damage of your main skill.
options.label: Ignore Skill Hit Damage?

options.tooltip: This option sets whether an enemy is between you and your linked target.
options.label: Is the enemy in your Link beams?

options.tooltip: This option sets whether or not the enemy has hit you with Fire Damage in the last 4 seconds.
options.label: Enemy hit you with Fire Damage?

options.tooltip: This option sets whether or not the enemy has hit you with Cold Damage in the last 4 seconds.
options.label: Enemy hit you with Cold Damage?

options.tooltip: This option sets whether or not the enemy has hit you with Lightning Damage in the last 4 seconds.
options.label: Enemy hit you with Light. Damage?

options.tooltip:
This overrides the default enemy level used to estimate your hit, evade chance, and damage reduction.

The maximum level for normal enemies and all bosses is 85.

The default level of normal enemies and bosses scales with player level unless manually set.

The default and minimum level for pinnacle bosses and uber pinnacle bosses is 82.
options.label: Enemy Level:

options.tooltip:
The enemy will automatically be considered to be Unique if they are a Boss,
but you can use this option to force it if necessary.
options.label: Is the enemy Rare or Unique?

options.tooltip:
Delirium scales enemy 'less Damage Taken' as well as enemy 'increased Damage dealt'
At 100% effect:
Enemies Deal 30% Increased Damage
Enemies take 80% Less Damage
options.label: Delirious Effect:

options.tooltip:
Enemy Maximum resistance is increased by the resistance configurations 
This locks it at the default value
options.label: Enemy Max Resistance is always 75%

options.tooltip:
The percentage of the roll range the enemy hits for 
 eg at 100% the enemy deals its maximum damage
options.label: Enemy Skill Roll Range %:

options.tooltip:
Controls which types of damage the EHP calculation uses:
	Average: uses the Average of all typed damage types (except Damage Over Time and Untyped)

If a specific damage type is selected, that will be the only type used.
options.label: Enemy Damage Type:
Enemy attack / cast time in ms:

options.tooltip: This multiplies the damage of a given skill in pvp, for instance any with damage multiplier specific to pvp (from skill or support or item like sire of shards)
options.label: Custom PvP Damage multiplier percent:

options.tooltip:
This overrides the default damage amount used to estimate your damage reduction from armour.
The default is 1.5 times the enemy's base damage, which is the same value
used in-game to calculate the estimate shown on the character sheet.
options.label: Enemy Skill Physical Damage:
